expected_findings.description: All parametrized test cases for merge_rayon_delta start from a zero-base log. A test merging into pre-existing non-zero counters is needed to catch accidental = instead of += in merge_rayon_delta, since 0 + x == x masks the bug.

The RayonCanonicalLogGuard exists specifically for panic safety, but no test verifies that a panic during evaluation cleans up the thread-local. A test using catch_unwind should assert the thread-local is clean after a panic.

merge_rayon_delta uses an explicit field list that must be kept in sync with any new evaluation counters. If someone adds a new counter used via with_canonical_log inside evaluate_single_flag but forgets to add it here, the original silent-loss bug is re-introduced for that field.
The comment says deltas are 'sent back through the oneshot channel' but the deltas are actually returned as part of the (flag, result, delta) tuple from the par_iter map closure.
